title: A Look At SoFi Technologies Valuation After Strong Q1 2026
text: SoFi Technologies posted record Q1 2026 earnings while facing valuation concerns from investors. The company achieved revenue of $1.1 billion (41% YoY growth), net income of $166.7 million, and added 1.1 million new members (35% YoY growth). Product cross-buy accelerated to 43%, with 1.8 million new products added to reach 22.2 million total. Despite these strong operational metrics, the stock declined significantly post-earnings. The market's concern centers on valuation, with the stock trading at approximately 27 times the 2026 earnings guidance of $0.60 per share. This premium valuation is elevated for a consumer lending-focused business vulnerable to economic downturns. Additional concerns include a 27% year-over-year decline in the technology platform segment due to a large customer departure and a 3.03% personal loan charge-off rate. However, the underlying business fundamentals remain healthy with strong revenue growth, improving profitability, and significant market opportunity. The company's guidance for 30% adjusted net revenue growth and $1.6 billion adjusted EBITDA suggests continued strong operational execution.
